description: baseline (e5-base, contrastive, bs=256, lr=1e-5, warmup_ratio=0.33, SHN pool placid-snake-749)
lr=2e-5 — peaked early at 0.6595 then regressed, overshoots short budget
bf16-mixed precision (+0.016, ~43% more steps in budget)
batch_size=512 (+0.007, more in-batch negatives)
batch_size=1024 OOM at 79GB
batch_size=768 also OOMs at 79GB
similarity_scale=30 (-0.004, too sharp)
max_offer_length=192 (-0.007, truncation cost > step gain)
gradient_checkpointing=true bs=1024 — only 950 steps, GC too slow
weight_decay=0.1 — within noise (+0.0001)
n_pos=1 n_neg=3 — hurts, 1 positive per query weakens contrastive signal
n_neg_samples_per_query=4 (+0.004, more SHN per anchor group)
n_neg_samples_per_query=6 — too few queries per batch
lr=5e-6 — undershoots
lr=1.5e-5 — overshoots at bs=512
fresh SHN pool mined from big-stoat-600 — my miner weaker than placid-snake-749 miner
infra: save_top_k=1 to limit disk usage (no metric impact)
warmup_steps=0 — warmup actually matters, no-warmup destabilizes
random_query_pool batching (+0.055, diverse in-batch negatives)
lr=2e-5 + RQP — within noise vs lr=1e-5
fresh SHN pool mined from defiant-mink-296 (+0.005)
output_dim=256 projection head (-0.015, projection hurts)
hard+semi-hard combo (-0.003, rank 0-20 has false positives)
iterative mine from valuable-cod-204 — plateaued
batch_size=640 — within noise, fewer steps cost outweighs larger batch gain
compile=false — within noise, compile marginally helps
